summary: SpaceX’s initial public offering has attracted demand for more than four times the available shares, according to people familiar with the matter, ahead of the Elon Musk-led rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence firm stopping taking orders.

title: SpaceX Owns a Real Business That Makes Big Money
summary: A simple view of SpaceX is that it’s a low-cost rocket launcher that created the profitable Starlink satellite business and which is now burning cash to build orbital data centers and colonize Mars. Starlink doesn’t have the lead role in SpaceX’s $1.8 trillion initial public offering. But it is the real business inside the company, and the telecoms industry and its regulators can ill afford to be complacent about the disruptive threat it poses.

title: Chinese Delivery Apps Are Taking Off in Brazil as Rivals Pile On Perks
summary: Regulatory scrutiny, alleged espionage and unfair competition claims are setting the tone for what was once just a battle for market share.

title: After China Sale, Starbucks Now Has Something Brewing in Japan
summary: The coffee giant is considering options for its Japanese business, which include a stake sale, following the disposal of a majority interest in its China retail operations.

title: ESG Currents: Malaysia’s EPF on Safeguarding Workers’ Retirement
summary: How does a sustainability focus support a pension fund’s duty to safeguard the retirement future of a country’s workers? What does effective engagement with companies look like? In this ESG Currents episode, Shahida Jaffar, Head of Corporate Sustainability at Malaysia’s Employees Provident Fund, joins Bloomberg Intelligence ESG analyst Conrad Tan to discuss the key principles guiding EPF’s engagement with investee companies and why it sees the defense of biodiversity and natural capital as a cri

title: Amazon’s Record Bond Deal Shakes Up Canada’s Credit Market
summary: Amazon.com Inc.’s record loonie bond sale has the Canadian fixed income market working hard to accommodate the deluge of debt, pushing risk spreads higher and spurring other borrowers to delay note sales of their own.

title: Oracle Earnings Pose Next Test for Suddenly Shaky AI Stock Rally
summary: The rally in artificial intelligence-related stocks is suddenly on shaky footing, and Oracle Corp.’s earnings report after the close represents the group’s next test.

title: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Tension Spurs Rally in Oil: Markets Wrap
summary: A flare-up in geopolitical tensions spurred a rally in oil and drove stocks lower, with Wall Street also rattled by a renewed selloff in some the world’s largest technology companies.

title: Warburg CEO Calls IPO Market ‘Broken’ Even Amid Giant Offerings
summary:
Bloomberg's Dani Burger speaks with Warburg Pincus CEO Jeffrey Perlman at the SuperReturn conference in Berlin. They discuss US IPOs as the current pipeline is now worth some $3.6 trillion.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sitting Out the SpaceX IPO? Why That’s Hard to Do
summary: Wall Street has been fixated on the stock market debut of Elon Musk’s SpaceX. The rocket-and-AI company’s initial public offering on June 12 is set to be the largest in history.

title: Ashley’s Frasers Seeks to Buy Rest of Boss for $2.3 Billion
summary: Frasers Group Plc offered to buy the rest of Hugo Boss AG for about €2 billion ($2.3 billion) as billionaire Mike Ashley seeks to add another fashion brand to his growing collection.

title: Drugmaker Parabilis Soars 67% After $745 Million IPO, Placement
summary: Parabilis Medicines Inc. shares jumped as much as 67% in its trading debut, after the company raised nearly $745 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced above its marketed range and a private placement.

title: SpaceX Holder Says Orbital Data-Centers Offer Big Payoff, Risks
summary: SpaceX’s $1.75 trillion initial public offering highlights the potential upside — and downside — from Elon Musk’s bold vision to place data centers in Earth orbit, according to one of the company’s private investors.

title: TrueCar's Scott Painter on Elon Musk and SpaceX
summary: SpaceX fever is spreading fast as investors rush into the record-breaking IPO. But before it became a global powerhouse, SpaceX was a startup fighting for survival. TrueCar founder and longtime Elon Musk adviser Scott Painter shares insight into Musk's entrepreneurial journey and SpaceX's early days on 'Bloomberg: Open Interest.' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Seanergy Sees Strength in Commodity Demand
summary: While markets watch the Strait of Hormuz, strong demand for coal, infrastructure materials, and other commodities continues to support dry bulk shipping. Stamatis Tsantanis, CEO of Seanergy Maritime and United Maritime, discusses the global demand outlook on 'Bloomberg Open Interest.' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 'There's A Ton of Dry Powder': Latham & Watkins' Kelly
summary: Alex Kelly, global co-chair of the M&A and Private Equity Practice at Latham & Watkins, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." They speak at the SuperReturn conference in Berlin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Amazon Inks $17.5 Billion Loan as AI Borrowing Frenzy Grows
summary: Soon after selling the biggest Canadian corporate bond sale on record, Amazon.com Inc. inked another multi-billion dollar financing as spending on artificial intelligence soars.

title: Lenovo on Strategy and Growth
summary: Winston Cheng, Senior Vice President & Chief Financial Officer, Lenovo said the AI infrastructure boom is probably “the greatest infrastructure build in the history of mankind,” when discussing company outlook with Bloomberg’s Sangmi Cha at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (This video has been edited to remove an incorrect graphic.) (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Extends Pullback as Trump Threatens More Strikes on Iran
summary: Gold extended declines after US President Donald Trump vowed to strike Iran again, rekindling concerns that oil disruptions will lead to higher interest rates.

title: Oil Crunch Is Stoking Asia’s Demand for Coal, Shipping CEO Says
summary: Coal is emerging as an increasingly attractive alternative to oil for energy-starved countries across Asia, according to Stamatis Tsantanis, chief executive officer of Seanergy Maritime Holdings Corp.

title: Subprime Auto Dealer America’s Car-Mart Seeks Rescue Funds
summary: America’s Car-Mart Inc., a used car seller and subprime lender, is working on an eleventh hour capital raise to stave off a potential bankruptcy filing after a cash crunch put the company on the verge of default, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Oil Jumps as Trump Threatens to ‘Hit Iran Hard’ Imminently
summary: Oil turned higher as President Donald Trump warned of imminent US attacks against Iran and domestic crude inventories posted another steep drop.

title: European Stocks Steady With ECB in Focus After US Inflation Data
summary: European stocks were muted as a key report on US inflation offered some relief to investors, with attention now turning to the European Central Bank’s policy decision.

title: Traders Keep Bets on a Fed Hike in 2026 After CPI Data
summary:
Bond traders maintained bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates by the end of the year, even after a soft US core inflation reading eased pressure on Chairman Kevin Warsh to act sooner. 
Sree Kochugovindan, Senior Research Economist at Aberdeen, discusses the path forward for the Fed, as well as what's in store for other central banks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pimco Warns a Wave of Defaults Is Coming for Low-Quality Borrowers
summary: Heavy spending on AI could widen economic outcomes and hit lower-quality loans, the firm says.

title: Canadian Bonds Rally After BOC Holds Rates, Cites Weak Economy
summary: Canadian government bonds rallied across the curve after the Bank of Canada held its policy interest rate steady and Governor Tiff Macklem described the economy as “weak.”

title: Commerzbank Raises More Concerns Tied to UniCredit Bid
summary: Commerzbank AG expressed fresh doubts whether the rising acceptance level of UniCredit SpA’s takeover bid is a full reflection of investor support.

title: Trump Says Citi Is the Top M&A Adviser, But It's Not
summary: President Donald Trump congratulated Citigroup and CEO Jane Fraser for being the top adviser on mergers and acquisitions. But the bank is nowhere near the top of the list in the first quarter and has actually slipped. Todd Gillespie covers the bank for us.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lula abre vantagem de 6 pontos sobre Flávio, diz Quaest
summary: O presidente Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva ampliou sua vantagem sobre o senador Flávio Bolsonaro depois que o adversário foi envolvido em um escândalo de corrupção e os EUA aumentaram a pressão sobre o Brasil às vésperas da eleição de outubro, mostra a pesquisa Genial/Quaest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/10/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: JPMorgan Sees May as CPI ‘High-Water Mark’ With Fed Set to Hold
summary: JPMorgan Asset Management’s David Kelly predicted the Federal Reserve will decide that the latest inflation data aren’t worrisome enough to prompt policymakers to do anything other than stand pat on interest rates next week.

title: US Inflation Picks Up to Three-Year High
summary:
US inflation accelerated in May to the fastest pace in more than three years as the Iran war pushed up energy prices, outstripping Americans’ pay gains.
Tiffany Wilding, Economist: North America at PIMCO, reacts to the May CPI report. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Blackstone's Brand on IPO Returns, Anthropic Partnership
summary: "The year of the IPO is definitely on," Martin Brand, head of Blackstone Capital Partners, says while speaking with Bloomberg's Dani Burger at the SuperReturn International Conference in Berlin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: MTN Finalizing Nigeria, Uganda Fintech Spinoffs; Adds Alipay
summary: MTN Group Ltd. is finalizing the spinoffs of its Nigerian and Ugandan financial-technology operations as part of a reorganization of its mobile-money unit to allow Mastercard Inc. and other strategic buyers to take minority stakes in its fintech businesses.
